title: My Biggest Mistake
content: You want to know my biggest mistake? [pause] In my forties, I got promoted. Big role. I threw myself into it completely. For about three years, I was physically present at home but mentally... I was still at work. My daughter was in high school, and I missed it. Not the events - I showed up to those. But the conversations. The moments. She'd try to talk to me and I'd be half-listening, thinking about the next meeting. [pause] My wife finally said: 'Joel, you're winning at work and losing at home.' That hit me hard. I made changes after that. But you don't get those years back. [pause] When people ask me about balance now? I don't give them theory. I give them that story.
follow_up: She's doing great now, my daughter. We're close. But I earned that closeness by finally showing up. Not by giving advice - by actually being present.
lesson: Being present isn't about time. It's about attention. And attention is a choice we make every moment.
